text:
Algerian boxer Imane Khelif made it clear that she was not bothered about the controversy surrounding her participation at theOlympicsafter storming into the women's 66kg gold medal match.Khelif cruised to a unanimous points victory over Janjaem Suwannapheng on Tuesday nightto guarantee herself at least a silver medal.

The 25-year-old has been at the centre of a media storm throughout the Olympics after previously failing a gender test last year. She was backed by hundreds of flag-waving Algerian supporters during her win over Suwannapheng and danced with delight as the judges' decision was read out.

Speaking after the bout, Khelif sent a bullish message to her critics as she insisted that she was not listening to the outside noise regarding her place at the Olympics.

Pick up your ultimate guide to the Olympics! Going for Gold provides the day-by-day rundown of what's on and when. Clickhereto buy.



"I don’t care," she said. "I don’t care what anyone is saying about me with the controversy. All that is important to me is that I stay on the level and give my people the performance they deserve. I know I’m a talented person and this is a gift to all Algerians."

Khelif will face Yang Liu in the gold medal match on Friday as she looks to finish her Olympic campaign by winning the biggest prize of all. She will be heavily backed by her Algerian fans when she takes to the ring against Yang, who beat Chen Nien-chin of Thailand in the semi-finals.

Her participation at the Olympics has sparked no shortage of controversy, given that she failed a gender test laid on by the International Boxing Association last year. The organisation is no longer recognised by the International Olympic Committee, who do not subject their athletes to the same testing.

Discovery+is the new home of the Olympics. The streamer is set to broadcast 3,800 hours of sport live from Paris.

Sports fans can getDiscovery+ for £3.99a month until the end of 2024 via Amazon Prime's video channels. This also comes with anAmazon Prime 30-day free trial.

Umar Kremlev, president of the IBA, allegedly claimed that Khelif's test displayed XY chromosomes but no evidence has ever been put forward. The Algerian fighter was born female, with her father providing documents last week to prove it following the spread of online misinformation.

Khelif, who has lost nine fights during her career, believes she has been the victim of bullying and said over the weekend: "I send a message to all the people of the world to uphold the Olympic principles and the Olympic Charter, to refrain from bullying all athletes, because this has effects, massive effects.

"It can destroy people, it can kill people’s thoughts, spirit and mind. It can divide people. And because of that, I ask them to refrain from bullying."

title: Imane Khelif sends bullish three-word message to critics after sealing Olympics final spot | Other | Sport | Express.co.uk
author: Archie Griggs
description: Imane Khelif will face Yang Liu in the women's 66kg gold medal match.
